title: 5 tips to protect yourself against (cyber)criminals this summer: "Holiday photos reveal more information than you think"

world.title: 5 tips to protect yourself against (cyber)criminals this summer: "Holiday photos reveal more information than you think"

environment.compositionIntent: A restrained Serra-like paper surround preserves active negative space while the accepted parts build the news meaning as low, measured pressure: a central serial black mass, orbit contours, vertical drags, and small pale data-leak interruptions.

distanceSafety.reason: Canonical public camera retained for the first full-world pass because all accepted isolated previews read as broad horizontal abstract fields with central pressure and no evidence required reframing.

contrast.notes: High-key paper light keeps the pale field open; a low dark rim preserves edge separation on serial panels, orbits, scratches, and drips.

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: background atmosphere, palette/value field, shallow depth, and active negative space
surrounding WebGPU-safe environment behavior that gives other parts room to read
avoid: main news-pressure band unless explicitly assigned
main knot or focal tangle unless explicitly assigned

compositionAdaptations: Kept at the particle/skybox-safe origin as the accepted surrounding background shell.
Opacity stays below full strength so the central pressure structure and foreground artist marks read against active pale negative space.
Kept at origin as the accepted pale paper environment and negative-space field.
composition modified world-environment from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected loose concept reference image directly: pale distressed paper field with serial dark masses, elliptical contour pressure, vertical drip/scratch density, and small pale interruptions; used only as advisory palette/value/mood guidance.
Static check passed for drafts/world-environment.module.mjs with no validation errors.

Preview attempt 2 rendered successfully in Playwright/WebGPU: desktop screenshot shows a single surrounding pale paper shell, stronger matte dark serial pressure panels, wide orbit contour lines, vertical paintstick drips, exposed pale cuts, and surrounding paper tooth speckle.

Render report for attempt 2 reports healthStatus ok, captureStatus ok, WebGPU renderer/backend proof, and WebGPU-safe feature evidence for MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, sky-background-field, color-field, procedural-marks, soft-particles, and utilities.createDirectionSpaceSkyboxShell.

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: middleground selected-news pressure structure: dominant band, grid compression, barricade, or equivalent pressure form
avoid: full foreground artist mark field of loops, scratches, drips, splatters, and near-line behavior

compositionAdaptations: Held near center as the dominant news-pressure structure: serial panels, central black weight, orbit contours, five pale interruptions, and vertical drag marks.
Scale is lifted after attempt 1 so the central black mass regains value authority on desktop and remains a readable pressure anchor in the mobile crop.
Held near center and enlarged in attempt 2 so the black pressure mass and orbit contours remain the primary news signal.
composition modified non-literal-orbits-pressure from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected the local concept-reference image directly as advisory evidence: pale paper ground, central black pressure mass, serial panels, orbit-like contours, and restrained vertical drag marks.

Playwright attempt 1 captured a nonblank desktop preview with a large central black Serra-like pressure mass, serial side panels, restrained orbit ribbons, five pale information cuts, and bounded vertical scars/grain.

Render report showed healthStatus ok, captureStatus ok, rendererMode webgpu, rendererBackend webgpu, and WebGPU-safe feature facts for MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, color-field, procedural-marks, and soft-particles.
Static check-part-draft passed with validation.pass true and no errors.

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: foreground artist mark and line behavior: loops, scratches, drips, splatters, dot/grid catches, and near readable marks
avoid: another dominant middleground news-pressure band, barricade/grid, or knot structure

compositionAdaptations: Kept in the declared foreground lane as near artist mark behavior: dragged loops, scratches, drips, splatter catches, and pale cuts.
Offset right/down and reduced again after attempt 1 so its pale support no longer washes over the central news-pressure band.
Reduced and shifted right/down in attempt 2 so foreground scratches and drips texture the scene without washing out the primary band.
composition modified artist-mark-field from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected concept reference directly: broad pale paper ground, black serial mass, looping contour pressure, vertical drips, and gritty mark density used only as advisory composition/value support.
Static check succeeded for drafts/artist-mark-field.module.mjs with no validation errors.

Attempt 2 Playwright desktop preview captured successfully and showed a readable foreground Serra-style mark field: pale paper support, thicker dragged matte black pigment, looped orbit contours, vertical drips, scratches, splatter/dot catches, and five pale information cuts.

Attempt 2 desktop render report status captured with healthStatus ok, WebGPU renderer/backend proof, WebGPU-safe standard materials, project material factory evidence, and approved animated-instanced-mark-field helper evidence.

owns: foreground artist mark and line behavior: loops, scratches, drips, splatters, dot/grid catches, and near readable marks

compositionEvidence.draftIntent: Preserve accepted WebGPU-compatible Serra modules and assemble them into an austere pale-paper field where holiday-photo data risk reads as a central black pressure mass, orbiting exposure paths, small pale information cuts, and a measured foreground scratch field.

previewDecision.summary: Attempt 2 is the final handoff: desktop preserves the full serial panel/orbit structure, mobile crops into the central pressure band with pale information cuts and vertical marks still visible, and both preview reports completed with healthStatus ok and captureStatus ok.
decisions: Canonical camera retained because both desktop and mobile previews kept the central pressure system readable without clipping or requiring portrait-specific reframing.
Primary news-pressure part was enlarged and held near center after attempt 1 so the black mass and orbit pressure remain the visual anchor.

Foreground artist-mark part was reduced and shifted right/down after attempt 1 because its pale paper support had softened the primary band; in attempt 2 it functions as line/scratch texture rather than a competing barricade.
Final grade uses restrained darker contrast to restore Serra-like value weight while preserving the pale paper field and WebGPU-safe material evidence from accepted modules.
